_meta.purpose: DRAFT prerequisite concept vocabulary + rules (spec docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-15-prerequisite-concept-graph-design.md §1A). Pending Tybalt's exhaustive review — rename to prereq_concepts.json only after approval. Consumed by scripts/import_course_concepts.py.
_meta.method: in-session multi-agent draft: 7 discipline-family proposers over the full in-scope inventory (4,730 CC courses, 230 UC receiving) -> synthesis -> 2 adversarial verifiers (coverage, rules); review doc at docs/prereq-concepts-review.md
conventions: Lab-only UC asks (e.g. PHYSICS 4AL, CHEM 1AL) are covered by their lecture concept; labs do not get separate concepts.
Corequisites are not encoded; where a relationship is commonly concurrent, encoding it as a completed prerequisite is a per-concept normative call recorded in that concept's note.
Combined courses (e.g. 'Linear Algebra and Differential Equations') map to BOTH constituent concepts.
Courses with no concept stay unmapped by design; concepts exist only for recurring pathway courses anchored by UC asks.
_meta.review: Approved 2026-07-15 by Tybalt ('that's fine, proceed'): decisions 1-5 stand as drafted (linear_alg<-calc_3 and diff_eq<-linear_alg are his stated normative calls; consequences accepted as documented); decision 6: software-tools cluster left unmapped for v1; decision 7: intro_stats/engl_comp_2 kept as CC-side concepts; decision 8: variance notes added post-approval. Combined-course convention for classification: a course spanning multiple concepts maps to the DEEPEST constituent in the partial order, flagged 'combined_course' (single-concept schema, v1); listed in QA for manual override.

concepts.name: Calculus I (single-variable, differential)
concepts.note: Entry point of the STEM chain; no precalc/trig concept exists because no such courses are in scope. Early/late-transcendentals, honors, and 'with Support' sections all fold in; split half-courses and combined Calc 1+2 packagings map jointly/multiply rather than earning new slugs.
example_cc_titles: Single Variable Calculus I - Early Transcendentals
Calculus with Analytic Geometry, First Course

concepts.name: Calculus II (integration, series)
concepts.note: Standard second course; no contested calls. Honors/support variants fold in.
example_cc_titles: Single Variable Calculus II - Late Transcendentals

concepts.name: Calculus III (multivariable / vector calculus)
concepts.note: NORMATIVE FOLD: one concept covers the whole multivariable+vector span; quarter-system 4th-course splits map jointly here (no calc_4). Also answers the separate vector-calculus asks (MATH 20E @ UCSD, MATH 6A @ UCSB) that semester CCs deliver inside one course.

concepts.name: Linear Algebra
concepts.note: NORMATIVE: requires calc_3 statewide even though many colleges gate on calc_2 or allow calc_3 concurrently — conservative upper bound per house rule. Combined 'Linear Algebra and Differential Equations' courses (~20 colleges) map to BOTH linear_alg and diff_eq, not a combined slug (anchored by MATH 54 @ UC Berkeley, MATH 024 @ UC Merced).

concepts.name: Ordinary Differential Equations
concepts.note: NORMATIVE: requires linear_alg (house call) even though most colleges gate ODE on calc_2/calc_3 with linear algebra at most a co-req; matches UCD MAT 022A->022B ordering and prevalence of combined LA+DE courses. calc_3 is inherited transitively; combined LA+DE courses map to both slugs.

concepts.name: Discrete Mathematics / Discrete Structures
concepts.note: MERGE of the math 'discrete_math' and cs 'discrete_math_cs' proposals into one concept (both are the C-ID COMP 152 / MATH idea; UC asks span MATH 61 @ UCLA and COMPSCI 70 @ Berkeley). NORMATIVE: requires calc_1 statewide for math-readiness; dropped the CS proposal's cs_1 edge because math-department discrete courses do not gate on programming, though CS-flavored 'Discrete Structures' variants that do gate on cs_1 still map here. Two-course discrete splits map jointly. Gating varies: many colleges gate discrete on precalc (out of scope -> source node) or list calc_1 as coreq/advisory; calc_1-completed is the conservative statewide call.

concepts.name: Applied/Business Calculus I
concepts.note: NORMATIVE FOLD: one track covers business, social-science, and life-science ('Calculus for Biology and Medicine') applied-calc variants; CC titles blend these audiences and the only distinct applied-calc UC ask is UCSC MATH 11A/11B. Does NOT satisfy calc_1 and does not feed the STEM chain; second entry point of the math graph.
example_cc_titles: Calculus for Business and Social Sciences
Basic Techniques of Applied Calculus I
Calculus for Biology and Medicine I

concepts.name: Applied/Business Calculus II
concepts.note: Second course of the applied track (San Diego 121/122, Peralta 16A/16B, Los Rios 355/356, etc.). Some colleges offer only the first course; life-science II variants fold in per the bus_calc_1 fold.
example_cc_titles: Calculus for Biology and Medicine II

concepts.name: Calculus-Based Probability & Statistics for STEM
concepts.note: MERGE of math 'stats_calc' and other 'prob_stats_calc' (same calc-based STEM/engineering prob-stats course). NORMATIVE: requires calc_2 as the defensible statewide floor — CC gates vary from calc_1 (UCM MATH 032) up to multivariable (PSTAT 120A). BOUNDARY: covers only calc-based courses; non-calc intro statistics is intro_stats.

concepts.name: Introductory Statistics (algebra-based)
concepts.note: C-ID MATH 110 / CCN STAT C1000 cluster (plus PSYC/BUS-prefixed equivalents), ~110 colleges. Distinct from stats_calc. No transfer-level requires (intermediate-algebra gate is pre-transfer). Answers stats asks at campuses that accept elementary stats in articulation.

concepts.name: Calculus-Based Physics: Mechanics
concepts.note: First course of the calc-based scientists-and-engineers sequence. NORMATIVE: requires calc_1 COMPLETED even though many colleges allow concurrent enrollment (corequisites are not encoded). Honors variants, attached labs, and combined 'Mechanics and Wave Motion' packagings all fold in.
example_cc_titles: Physics for Scientists and Engineers: Mechanics
Classical Mechanics for Scientists and Engineers

concepts.name: Calculus-Based Physics: Electricity & Magnetism
concepts.note: NORMATIVE: requires calc_2 completed; a large minority also require calc_3 concurrently, deliberately NOT encoded to avoid overstating the floor and double-counting the math chain. Treated as second-in-sequence statewide even where a campus folds E&M elsewhere in its quarter packaging (UCB 7B, UCI 7D).
example_cc_titles: Physics for Scientists and Engineers: Electricity and Magnetism

concepts.name: Calculus-Based Physics: Waves, Thermodynamics & Optics
concepts.note: Third course (fluids/waves/heat/optics; at 3-course colleges also absorbs modern-physics content). NORMATIVE serialization: requires phys_em even though several colleges allow it directly after mechanics — one defensible statewide total order is preferred. Anchors are sequence-positional, not content-exact, since campus packaging varies.
example_cc_titles: Physics for Scientists and Engineers: Fluids, Waves, Optics and Thermodynamics
Heat, Waves, Light and Modern Physics

concepts.name: Calculus-Based Physics: Modern Physics
concepts.note: Fourth course at 4-course colleges. NORMATIVE: requires phys_waves_thermo (conservative — some colleges gate on E&M only). At 3-course colleges modern-physics content lives inside phys_waves_thermo and no local course maps here; that asymmetry is intentional.
example_cc_titles: Modern Physics for Scientists and Engineers
Physics for Scientists and Engineers: Modern Physics

concepts.name: General Physics I (Algebra/Trig-Based)
concepts.note: First semester of the non-calculus sequence for life-science majors. EDGE DROPPED: the proposal's requires 'precalc' has no concept in scope (no precalc/trig courses in the inventory), so this is a source node. Lecture + separate 1u lab pairs map jointly.

concepts.name: General Physics II (Algebra/Trig-Based)
concepts.note: Second semester (E&M, optics, modern). NORMATIVE: the CC side is a 2-semester sequence answering UCSB's 3-quarter 6A-6C ask, so no third algebra-based concept; rare 'General Physics III' offerings map here or stay unmapped.
example_cc_titles: General Physics - Electricity, Magnetism and Optics

concepts.name: General Chemistry I
concepts.note: Entry point of the chem chain. NORMATIVE: no in-vocabulary math prerequisite — the standard gate is intermediate-algebra/quant-reasoning readiness (pre-transfer, no concept); minority precalc/prep-chem gates deliberately not modeled. Lecture/lab split offerings map jointly onto this single lecture+lab concept; honors variants map identically.
example_cc_titles: General Chemistry and Chemical Analysis I

concepts.name: General Chemistry II
concepts.note: Completes the full-year sequence that quarter UCs express as A/B/C. NORMATIVE: no distinct gen_chem_3 — third-quarter courses at the few quarter-calendar CCs fold in here as the terminal element. 'General Chemistry and Qualitative Analysis' titles are ordinary second-semester courses and map here.

concepts.name: Organic Chemistry I
concepts.note: NORMATIVE: gated on the FULL YEAR of general chemistry (gen_chem_2) statewide even though some receiving courses (UCB CHEM 3A needs only 1A) and a few CCs admit after Gen Chem I — the full-year gate is the dominant CC pattern and the conservative choice. Lecture/lab splits map jointly.
example_cc_titles: Organic Chemistry for Science Majors I

concepts.name: Organic Chemistry II
concepts.note: Completes the full-year organic sequence. NORMATIVE: no distinct organic_chem_3 — third-quarter organic at quarter-calendar CCs folds in here as the terminal element. Lecture/lab splits map jointly.
example_cc_titles: Organic Chemistry for Science Majors II

concepts.name: Programming Fundamentals (CS1)
concepts.note: NORMATIVE: one language-agnostic CS1 concept — Python, Java, C++, and C intro courses all map here (UCs articulate them interchangeably). CS0 survey/computer-literacy courses do NOT map here. Honors variants and co-listed labs map with the parent; two-course CS1 splits map jointly.
example_cc_titles: Programming Concepts and Methodology I: C++
Structure and Interpretation of Computer Programs

concepts.name: Intermediate / Object-Oriented Programming (CS2)
concepts.note: NORMATIVE: three-link programming chain (cs_1 -> cs_2_oop -> cs_3_data_structures) because the modal CA sequence is three courses; UCI/Davis/Merced/UCSB each ask for a distinct intermediate/OOP course BEFORE data structures. Language-agnostic; 'Advanced Objects', 'Intermediate Java', 'C++ Programming 2' map here.
example_cc_titles: Programming Concepts and Methodology II: C++

concepts.name: Data Structures & Algorithms (CS3)
concepts.note: NORMATIVE/CONSERVATIVE: requires cs_2_oop statewide even though a minority articulate CS1 -> DS directly (same philosophy as linear_alg requiring calc_3); such DS courses still map here and inherit the longer chain. UCLA COM SCI 32 is answered by cs_2_oop + cs_3_data_structures together; separate 'Advanced Data Structures' add-ons stay unmapped.

concepts.name: Computer Organization & Assembly Language
concepts.note: NORMATIVE: requires only cs_1, the dominant sending-side prereq for C-ID COMP 142-style courses, even though several UC counterparts sit AFTER data structures (COMPSCI 61C, UCSD CSE 30, COM SCI 33) — we sequence by CC prereqs, not UC placement. Deliberately the looser variant here because the looser prereq is modal (recorded). Two-course assembly splits map jointly.

concepts.name: C / Systems Programming (second language)
concepts.note: NORMATIVE: kept because four campuses ask for a distinct 'C after a first language' or systems-in-C course (UCI, UCSC, UCSD, Davis). A C course that is a college's FIRST programming course maps to cs_1, not here; this slug is only for second-language/systems C presuming prior programming. Requires cs_1 (modal CC prereq).
example_cc_titles: C as a Second Programming Language

concepts.name: Digital Logic Design
concepts.note: MERGE of the cs and engr 'digital_logic' proposals into one slug (UC asks straddle CS and EE departments; UCLA COM SCI M51A is cross-listed EC ENGR M16). NORMATIVE: college prereqs vary from none (UCI EECS 31, UCSB ECE 15A are open freshman courses) to a first programming course (UCLA CS M51A); we conservatively gate on cs_1 statewide per house doctrine. OR-edges (accepting engr_programming as the qualifying course) are not expressible, so the edge is cs_1 only.

concepts.name: Cell and Molecular Biology (Majors)
concepts.note: Canonical FIRST course of the majors biology sequence. NORMATIVE: no requires-edge to gen_chem_1 even though general chemistry commonly appears on the prereq line — most colleges treat CHEM 1A as coreq/recommended-concurrent, not a completed predecessor. Integrated lecture+lab courses satisfy the UC's separate intro-bio-lab ask; no standalone majors-bio-lab concept exists in scope.
example_cc_titles: Principles of Biology - Molecular and Cellular

concepts.name: Organismal, Evolutionary and Ecological Biology (Majors)
concepts.note: SECOND course of the majors sequence, deliberately ONE node absorbing organismal + evolution + ecology + biodiversity + form/function/physiology however colleges package it (single course, Botany+Zoology pair, or 3-course split whose 2nd/3rd both map here). NORMATIVE: requires bio_cell_molec — cell-first is dominant and we take the stricter edge per house policy even though some colleges allow either order.

concepts.name: Human Physiology
concepts.note: Dedicated single-term Human Physiology (majors/pre-professional, anchoring UCB MCELLBI 32). NORMATIVE: requires bio_cell_molec AND gen_chem_1 — physiology builds on completed biology plus chemistry; conservative call requires full general chemistry and the majors cell/molecular course even though some allied-health sections accept lighter prereqs. Multi-semester A&P sequences are NOT mapped here.

concepts.name: Introductory Circuit Analysis for Engineers
concepts.note: The single dominant engr transfer course statewide (~50 colleges). NORMATIVE: most colleges allow diff_eq as a COREQUISITE (a minority gate only on calc_2) but we conservatively require diff_eq COMPLETED; calc-based E&M (phys_em) is required and the calc chain underneath is inherited, not duplicated. Lecture+lab pairs map to this ONE concept; 'Network Analysis', 'Circuits and Devices', 'Introduction to Electrical Engineering' titles fold in.

concepts.name: Programming and Computational Methods for Engineers
concepts.note: One concept covering the MATLAB flavor and the C/C++/Python-for-engineers flavor. NORMATIVE: requires calc_1 COMPLETED even though many colleges list it as coreq/advisory (conservative gate). Courses that are plainly a generic CS1 in an ENGR prefix map to cs_1 instead; this slug is reserved for the engineering-applications flavor. Only pure engr-side UC ask is UCR EE 20B.
example_cc_titles: Computational Methods for Engineers and Scientists
Introduction to Programming Concepts and Methodologies for Engineers

concepts.name: English Composition 1 (Academic Reading & Writing)
concepts.note: CCN ENGL C1000 / Cal-GETC 1A — the most standardized course in the state (~110 colleges). NORMATIVE: all first-composition variants map here — honors, stretch/extended-support, and ESL/multilingual equivalents. No transfer-level prerequisite encoded (placement gates are pre-transfer).
example_cc_titles: ENGL C1000 Academic Reading and Writing
ENGL C1000E Academic Reading and Writing (extended)
College Reading and Composition I Plus Lab
ESL 110 College Composition for Non-Native Speakers

concepts.name: English Composition 2 (Critical Thinking & Composition)
concepts.note: CCN ENGL C1001 / Cal-GETC 1B. NORMATIVE: ONE concept covers every comp-2 flavor — ENGL C1001, literature-based critical-thinking comp, and PHIL 'Critical Thinking and Writing' — because they fill the same 1B slot. Pure logic/argumentation without a composition component stays unmapped. Anchor is the systemwide Cal-GETC 1B ask plus its role as gate into the literature concepts.
example_cc_titles: ENGL C1001 Critical Thinking and Writing
Critical Thinking and Writing about Literature
Argumentative Writing and Critical Thinking Through Literature

concepts.name: Public Speaking (Oral Communication)
concepts.note: CCN COMM C1000 / Cal-GETC 1C; ~110 colleges. Only explicit receiving anchor is Davis CMN 001, but 1C makes it a de facto statewide ask. NORMATIVE: argumentation/debate, persuasion, rhetorical criticism, and advanced public speaking do NOT map here — first-course public speaking only.
example_cc_titles: COMM C1000 Introduction to Public Speaking

concepts.name: Principles of Microeconomics
concepts.note: C-ID ECON 201 family, ~110 colleges. NORMATIVE: no requires edge between micro and macro in either direction — colleges disagree on 1A/1B ordering, so encoding one would be indefensible. No math gate encoded (typical prereqs are pre-transfer algebra).

concepts.name: Principles of Macroeconomics
concepts.note: C-ID ECON 202 family, ~110 colleges. Same normative calls as econ_micro: no micro<->macro ordering, no math gate. Combined one-course 'Introduction to Economics' surveys stay unmapped rather than force-fit.

concepts.name: Financial Accounting
concepts.note: Largest single cluster (~120 colleges, C-ID ACCT 110) and the direct sending counterpart of UCR BUS 20. Managerial accounting does not appear in the inventory and is not minted; vocational Bookkeeping I/II stays unmapped.

concepts.name: Introduction to Literature
concepts.note: ~40 colleges; direct sending counterpart of Davis ENL 003. Covers 'Introduction to Literature', 'Approaches to Literature', and genre-survey courses. Requires engl_comp_1 per the near-universal college prereq. Comp-2-through-literature courses carrying the 1B function map to engl_comp_2 instead.

concepts.name: World Literature I (Antiquity to ~1650)
concepts.note: ~30 colleges. Answers Davis COM 001/COM 002; era mapping is approximate (one CC course may articulate to one or both). No ordering edge to world_lit_2: the halves are parallel, not sequential, at nearly all colleges. Comp-1 gate varies (some colleges use comp-2 or placement eligibility); comp-1-completed is the conservative call, matching intro_lit.
example_cc_titles: Masterpieces of World Literature I: 1500 BCE - 1600 CE
Survey of World Literature to the Mid-Seventeenth Century

concepts.name: World Literature II (~1650 to Present)
concepts.note: ~30 colleges. Answers Davis COM 003/COM 004; contemporary-world-literature variants fold in here as the closest COM 004 counterparts rather than getting their own slug. Comp-1 gate varies (some colleges use comp-2 or placement eligibility); comp-1-completed is the conservative call, matching intro_lit.
example_cc_titles: Masterpieces of World Literature II: 1600 - Present
World Literature II: Seventeenth Century Through the Present
